title: luzula campestris subsp. pallescens
intro: Luzula campestris subsp. pallescens, also known as pale woodrush, is a plant species belonging to the family Juncaceae, characterized by its yellowish-green foliage and small, brownish-purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of luzula campestris subsp. pallescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various health concerns. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its medicinal compounds.
